semanticCore: Sheath, covering, envelope, sleeve, bud of a flower, or mask
quranicRelevance: Date-palms with sheaths/flower buds (Akmaam - 'thatin al-akmaam', 'min akmaamiha').

keyDerivations.meaning: Sheath, flower bud, cover of fruit, protective envelope, or sleeve

figurativeOrRareUsages: وَالنَّخْلُ ذَاتُ الْأَكْمَامِ (55:11): And date-palms producing sheaths/flower buds (the initial protective layer covering date clusters).
summaryEn: The root represents protective sheaths, describing the natural envelopes (Akmaam) that cover date palm clusters and growing fruits.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر الغلاف والأوعية الواقية للثمار وبراعم الورد (كم)، واصفاً أوعية ثمار النخيل والطلع التي تنشق لتخرج الرطب (ذات الأكمام).
rawText: كَمٌّ and كَمٌ are both app. right: see قَدْ. كَمِّيَّةٌ Quantum, or quantity, as answering to “ how many. ” كَمْ : see its syn. كَأَىٍّ or كَأَيِّنْ voce أَىٌّ. === As an interrogative with the specification suppressed, What number? How many? and, as in an ex. voce سِقْىٌ, virtually meaning How much? and so in an ex. voce مَسَافَةٌ and voce شَبْرٌ. Also How long? as in the Kur, ii. 161, &c.; a noun signifying a period of time being understood: see also an ex. voce عَسَفَ, and voce فَرَشَ. كَمْكَامٌ The cancamum-tree: see بُطْمٌ and ضِرْوٌ. كُمَّةٌ A قَلَنْسُوَة: (TA in art. بطح:) or a round قلنسوة: (S, K:) pl. كِمَامٌ. (K, * TA in art. بطح.) كِمَامَةٌ : see كِمٌّ. كِمٌّ The calyx of a flower. (K, &c.) === كِمٌّ The envelope [or spathe] of the طَلْع [or spadix of a palm-tree]: and the covering [or calyx] of flowers or blossoms; as also 🡻كِمَامَةٌ. (S, Msb, K.) مُكَمَّمٌ Covered over, or concealed (مَسْتُور). (S, art. خصب.) See بُرْأَة.
